Key differences:

- Distractor 1 has 0 filled columns on the left (3 empty columns), 2 filled columns on the right
- Distractor 2 has 2 filled columns on the left, 1 empty column in the middle, 2 filled columns on the right

So the target is unique in that:
- It has exactly one filled column on the left side
- It has two consecutive empty columns in the middle
- The first column is filled while columns 2 and 3 are empty

I could describe it as:
- "A single filled column on the left, followed by two empty columns, then two filled columns on the right"
- "The leftmost column is filled, the next two columns are empty, and the rightmost two columns are filled"


- "One filled column, two empty columns, then two filled columns"

The most distinctive aspect is that the target has exactly one filled column on the left, whereas distractor 1 has none and distractor 2 has two. Since all three share the same right side pattern, the left portion is what sets the target apart.
